title: torilis helvetica proles neglecta
intro: Torilis helvetica proles neglecta, also known as wild chervil or hedge parsley, is a flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, due to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. Torilis helvetica proles neglecta can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat the aforementioned health issues.
